text:
There once was a little owl whom nobody liked. You see, he didn't care about parties or games. Birds just didn't think he could talk. But then one moonlit night a wise old owl fluttered his wings. Oh! What an occasion! Everyone wanted stories. And so did the little owl.

He told them how he loved ancient woods. Leaves whispering ancient secrets. Birds their beloved kin. Even snakes. You have to judge them. Anyway, he even sang. His song was lovely. Maybe too lovely. Birds flew into his feathers. But soon they sang too. And a small crowd gathered. Even they were enchanted.

The little owl didn't talk. But his eyes... oh. They saw. Oh. How beautiful. Something miraculous happened. Birds joined.

For days. Weeks. A small forest came alive. Little owlets were brave. Owls slept. Tiny friends. Even the littlest bird liked our hero.

One evening, a big owl flew. A
